name: Run lifecycle stages
description: Every run follows 14 stages in order: config_validation, environment_validation, model_resolution, dataset_resolution, dataset_profiling, tokenization_preprocessing, training_preparation, adapter_attachment, training_start, training_progress, evaluation, checkpoint_save, artifact_finalization, completion. Stages 10-12 may repeat. All stages visible in UI.

name: Atomic checkpoint writes
description: Checkpoints written atomically: write to temp dir (checkpoints/.tmp-checkpoint-{step}/), then atomic rename to final path (checkpoints/checkpoint-{step}/). If process crashes during write, .tmp- prefixed directory is cleaned up on next startup. Prevents referencing partially-written checkpoints.

name: Watchdog heartbeat monitoring
description: Trainer writes heartbeat file every N seconds (default 10s) at projects/{project_name}/.heartbeat with run_id, pid, current_step, timestamp, stage, metrics. Watchdog checks PID via psutil. If heartbeat stale (>120s default) AND PID dead, transitions run to failed. On startup, crash recovery checks all running runs.

title: SFT with LoRA/QLoRA as primary training mode
rationale: Practical on local hardware, resource-aware, common workflow, easier to observe and compare, well-supported by PEFT and trl libraries. Uses HuggingFace trl.SFTTrainer with custom callback hooks for real-time metric emission, stage transitions, checkpoint management, activation sampling, and heartbeat updates.

name: Tiered activation snapshots
description: Tier 1 (always captured): statistical summaries — mean, std, min, max, histogram bins (~1KB/layer/snapshot), stored in SQLite activation_snapshots.summary_json. Tier 2 (on explicit user request): full tensors saved as .pt files to filesystem, path in activation_snapshots.full_tensor_path. Balances observability with storage cost.
